database_info.name: UTP Cable & Infrastructure Selection Theory
database_info.purpose: Engineering design tool for structured cabling selection in surveillance and IP systems

1_conductor_material_selection.principle: Conductor material determines signal quality, PoE performance, and maximum distance

comparison.poe_performance: Best — lowest voltage drop, full 100m PoE
comparison.best_for: PoE cameras (especially PoE+/Hi-PoE), long runs (80-100m), enterprise
comparison.standard_compliance: Fully compliant with TIA-568.2-D and EN 50173

comparison.signal_quality: Good — passes Cat6 data tests at shorter distances
comparison.best_for: Budget installations, short runs, non-PoE or light PoE
comparison.standard_compliance: Does NOT meet TIA-568 permanent link spec (TIA requires solid copper). Acceptable per some regional standards.

1_conductor_material_selection.recommendation: For PoE cameras: ALWAYS use pure copper (OFC) for runs >60m. CCA is acceptable for patch cords and short runs <60m. For PoE+ and Hi-PoE PTZ cameras: pure copper MANDATORY.

2_indoor_vs_outdoor_cable.principle: Cable jacket must match installation environment

options.best_for: Inside buildings, conduit, cable tray, riser
options.note: Most common. Low smoke versions (LSZH) available for plenum.

options.best_for: Aerial, underground (in conduit), exposed outdoor runs
options.note: PE jacket resists UV degradation and moisture. Use gel-filled for direct burial.

options.jacket: PVC or LSZH with foil shield
options.best_for: High-EMI environments (near power cables, motors, fluorescent lights), healthcare, industrial
options.note: Requires shielded connectors and grounded patch panels for shield to be effective.

3_cat6_performance.principle: Cat6 provides headroom over Cat5e for surveillance applications

specs.channel_composition: 90m permanent link + 5m patch cord each end
specs.crosstalk: Better NEXT/FEXT than Cat5e — important for PoE and gigabit

why_cat6_for_surveillance: Future-proof: supports 1Gbps now, 10Gbps for short runs later
Better PoE: lower crosstalk heating allows higher PoE power delivery

4_patch_cord_color_coding.principle: Color coding improves cable management and troubleshooting

recommended_scheme.use: PoE devices (non-camera: access control, intercom, WAP)
Fire alarm / life safety network (if applicable)

4_patch_cord_color_coding.note: Adopt a consistent scheme and document it. Any scheme is acceptable — consistency is what matters.

5_patch_cord_length_selection.principle: Patch cord length should minimize excess cable while allowing movement

guide.use: Switch to patch panel in same rack unit
Switch to patch panel within same rack (1-2U apart)
Switch to patch panel in adjacent rack, or camera tail in ceiling void
Camera to wall plate, or floor-to-desk run
Longer desk runs, equipment to distant patch panel
Temporary connections, testing, long equipment runs

5_patch_cord_length_selection.rule: Use shortest practical length. Excess cable in switch rack creates airflow obstruction and messy cable management.

6_poe_cable_considerations.principle: PoE generates heat in cable — derated in bundles and high ambient temperature

poe_heating.issue: PoE current causes resistive heating in cable conductors. Bundles amplify heat.
poe_heating.tia_tsb_184a: Reduce maximum channel length in large bundles carrying PoE

derating_guide.note: 20% derating — use larger conduit for cooling

voltage_drop.example: PoE camera 0.3A on CCA cable (12Ω/100m): V_drop at 90m = 0.3 × 12 × 2 × 0.9 = 6.48V. From 48V PoE: 48 - 6.48 = 41.52V remaining (min required: 37V) — marginal!
voltage_drop.pure_copper_example: Same camera on OFC (9.38Ω/100m): V_drop = 0.3 × 9.38 × 2 × 0.9 = 5.07V. 48 - 5.07 = 42.93V — comfortable.
voltage_drop.conclusion: Pure copper cable has 25-30% less voltage drop → more reliable PoE at distance.

T568A.pairs: W/G, G, W/O, B, W/B, O, W/Br, Br

T568B.pairs: W/O, O, W/G, B, W/B, G, W/Br, Br

wiring_schemes.rule: Use ONE scheme consistently throughout entire installation. T568B is most common. Never mix A and B on the same link.

testing.tool: Fluke DSX or equivalent Cat6 cable certifier
testing.pass_criteria: All parameters must pass Cat6 permanent link or channel limits per TIA-568.2-D

8_cable_pathway_design.principle: Cable pathways must protect cables and maintain bend radius

requirements.conduit_fill: 40% maximum fill ratio (cable area / conduit area)
requirements.fire_stop: Fire-rated penetration seals required where cables pass through fire-rated walls/floors

9_quantity_estimation.principle: Estimate cable quantity with waste factor before ordering

formulas.cable_per_camera: Average run length = (shortest + longest) / 2 + 3m (slack at each end)
formulas.total_cable_m: sum(all camera run lengths) × 1.10 waste factor
formulas.patch_cords: 2 per camera (1 at switch, 1 at camera/patch panel) + 10% spares

9_quantity_estimation.example: 16 cameras, average run 50m: 16 × (50+3) × 1.10 = 933m → 4 boxes of 305m (1,220m total with margin)

10_compliance.principle: Structured cabling must meet performance and fire safety standards

standards.scope: Generic cabling — general requirements (European equivalent of TIA-568)
Fire class rating for cables in buildings (Eca, Dca, Cca, B2ca)
US cable safety listing for communications and plenum cables
